mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding economic activity and rising household income, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS growth do not exceed 50% hit rate over any 3-year rolling window, or t-test of mean excess return versus zero yields p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.0% reflects restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and corporate debt-service costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and subdued year-over-year consumer price inflation below 3.0% (CPIAUCSL) indicate strong real demand without imminent monetary tightening; this joint condition drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month increase > 0 AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year change < 3.0%
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform the sum of independent single-series signals over a 2-year rolling window, or incremental Rank IC from the interaction term is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor markets and impending economic contraction, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are not negative in more than 50% of triggered instances during late-cycle periods, or the signal hit rate is indistinguishable from random (binomial p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects contemporaneous real output expansion and corporate earnings momentum, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive INDPRO growth show no statistically significant excess return versus zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening residential investment and aggregate demand, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not negative in more than 50% of triggered observations, or mean return is positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) and decelerating nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) capture monetary policy overkill that restricts economic activity, jointly forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS month-over-month increase > 0 AND PAYEMS month-over-month change < prior 3-month average
failure_condition: The interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power over the main effects (F-test p > 0.05) in a rolling 3-year regression of 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeding 3.5% triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 45% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window, or mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
